title: San Blas Boat Rental: Tips, Costs and Best Yachts for 2026
html:
San Blas, an archipelago of 365 islands off Panama's Caribbean coast, offers some of the finest bareboat and crewed charters in Central America. Sailing from Cartí or Porvenir, you can rent a yacht for a week from around €2,500 in low season. For the widest choice, compare verified listings on boat hire San Blas through BoatHire24.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in San Blas?

Expect to pay between €200 and €4,000 per day depending on boat type and season. Shared day trips cost as little as €120 per person, while a private catamaran for eight people runs from €1,500. Your exact quote will depend on duration, crew requirements, and whether you visit the outer islands such as Isla Perro or Coco Bandero.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in San Blas?

Panamanian law requires a valid boating licence for bareboat charters, but most operators accept an ICC or equivalent. For crewed charters you need no licence, only a passport. Always confirm with the operator before booking.

Where to set off in San Blas

The main departure points are the small ports of Cartí and Porvenir on the mainland coast, both with regular boat traffic to the islands. Other common starting points include the town of Puerto Obaldía near the Colombian border and the anchorage at Isla Tigre. The bay of Playón Chico also serves as a hub for guided tours. Navigation is straightforward between the islands, but you should keep a lookout for coral heads.

Which boat to rent

  1. Motor yacht, best for fast island-hopping, up to 8 guests, from €1,200 per day. The Princess V58 is a popular choice; see the Princess V58 listing for full specs.
  2. Sailing catamaran, great for families and stability, up to 10 guests, from €900 per day.
  3. Monohull sailboat, ideal for traditional cruising, up to 6 guests, from €600 per day.
  4. Speedboat or RIB, perfect for half-day excursions, up to 8 guests, from €350.

Cost breakdown: what you pay in San Blas

  • Shared day tour (6, 8 guests): €120, €200 per person.
  • Small motorboat half-day (up to 4 guests): from €200.
  • Catamaran full-day (up to 10 guests): from €1,200.
  • Crewed yacht including captain and cook (up to 8 guests): from €1,800 per day.

Best season and local conditions

The high season runs from December to April, when the trade winds, the Alisios, blow steadily from the north-east at 15, 20 knots. From May to November you can expect more rain and lighter winds, but prices drop by up to 30 per cent.

Anchor in the lee of an island to avoid the Alisio swell; the sandbars near Isla Perro offer calm swimming but check the tide.

At a glance

  • Price band: €120, €4,000 per day
  • Peak months: December, April
  • Typical trip length: 4 hours to 7 days
  • Capacity: 4, 12 guests
  • Currency: EUR (prices quoted in euros)

Questions about boat rental in San Blas

Can you rent a boat without a captain in San Blas?

Yes, bareboat charters are available for licence holders, but first-time visitors often prefer a skipper given the coral-strewn passages.

What is the best boat for a family of six?

A Lagoon 42 sailing catamaran provides four double cabins, a saloon and ample deck space.

How far is it from Cartí to the prime snorkelling spots?

The distance to Isla Perro is about 12 nautical miles, a 45-minute ride in a speedboat.

Are there any restrictions on anchoring overnight?

Anchoring is generally allowed anywhere outside the Kuna Yala protected zones; always check with your charter company.

Whether you plan a day trip or a week-long voyage, BoatHire24 connects you with trusted operators across the archipelago. Owners can also list your boat and reach thousands of travellers seeking the perfect San Blas charter.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in San Blas and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
